title: daphne bholua subsp. emeiensis
intro: Daphne bholua subsp. emeiensis, also known as the Emei daphne or Emei bholua, is a subspecies of Daphne bholua, a flowering plant in the family Thymelaeaceae. It is native to the Sichuan province of China and has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and providing antioxidant protection against cell damage. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
